title: dryas integrifolia subsp. sylvatica
intro: Dryas integrifolia subsp. sylvatica, also known as the alpine dryad or mountain dryad, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Dryas integrifolia subsp. sylvatica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or salves, which are often used to treat skin irritations, cold sores, and other external health issues.
